selected_topic: First-ever dinosaur fossil discovered in Antarctica reveals a titanosaur
news_angle: Scientists have unearthed the first known dinosaur fossil from Antarctica, identifying it as a titanosaur, which sheds new light on prehistoric life in polar regions.
why_this_is_news: This discovery represents a landmark paleontological find, revealing that titanosaurs roamed Antarctica and providing fresh insights into dinosaur distribution and climate adaptation.
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao as an intrepid explorer cat in stylish streetwear, wielding a fossil-hunting tool on an icy Antarctic landscape with dinosaur bones scattered around, eyes gleaming with excitement and mischief
image_prompt: A charismatic young athletic orange tabby cat with sharp tabby markings and a confident grin, dressed in black hoodie, cap, sunglasses, and sneakers, posed dynamically amid snowy Antarctic terrain, uncovering a gigantic dinosaur femur fossil with icy mountains and a pale blue sky background, animated style, cinematic lighting, vibrant colors, energetic and adventurous atmosphere
easter_eggs: hidden paw print in the dinosaur bone texture
small Pangaea globe in the background
tiny cartoonish Antarctic penguin watching with curiosity
caption: Researchers unearthed the first-ever dinosaur fossil in Antarctica, identifying it as a massive titanosaur. Hunk Mao’s ready to lead the next polar dig – fossils and street cred both intact.
risk_notes: Ensure the Antarctic setting avoids generic icy tropes by integrating realistic fossil elements and scientific accuracy in the dinosaur depiction.
